name: Hacienda La Esmeralda
tasting_notes: Intensely floral with jasmine and bergamot, cascading into tropical fruit — ripe mango, lychee, and papaya. Extraordinary clarity and length.
awards: Best of Panama Competition winner multiple times

name: Finca El Injerto
tasting_notes: Complex and structured with notes of dark chocolate, black cherry, and a crisp malic acidity.
awards: Cup of Excellence winner

name: Yirgacheffe Cooperative
tasting_notes: Delicate and tea-like with distinct notes of lemon, jasmine, and bergamot.
awards: Recognized by SCA for consistent quality over multiple harvests

name: Beloya Station
tasting_notes: A quintessential natural Ethiopian profile bursting with strawberry jam, blueberry, and milk chocolate.
awards: Awarded at the Ethiopia National Coffee Competition

name: Kieni Factory
tasting_notes: Incredibly vibrant and juicy. Explodes with blackcurrant, rhubarb, and pink grapefruit. Syrupy body.
awards: Multiple top-10 finishes at Kenya Cup of Excellence

name: Finca Hartmann
tasting_notes: Sweet and fruit-forward with winey acidity. Notes of cherry, dark rum, and cacao.
awards: Best of Panama Competition finalist; recognised by SCA

name: Dota Cooperative
tasting_notes: Clean, crisp, and exceedingly sweet. Notes of green apple, caramel, and milk chocolate.
awards: Carbon-neutral certified; Costa Rica Excellence Award 2019

name: La Palma y El Tucán
tasting_notes: Innovative processing leads to complex flavors of tropical fruits, yogurt, and vibrant florals.
awards: Best Colombian Micro-Mill at Colombia Cup of Excellence 2020

name: Fazenda Santa Inês
tasting_notes: Supremely sweet and creamy with notes of hazelnut, milk chocolate, and dried fig.
awards: Cup of Excellence winner

name: Aida Batlle farms
tasting_notes: Refined and elegant with notes of nectarine, honey, and hibiscus.
awards: Cup of Excellence winner

name: Finca Las Ranas
tasting_notes: Balanced and sweet with milk chocolate, orange zest, and almond.
awards: Guatemala Cup of Excellence finalist

name: Finca La Esperanza
tasting_notes: Clean and bright with notes of green apple and toffee.
awards: Honduras Cup of Excellence Top 20

name: Bensa Shantawene
tasting_notes: Floral and fruit-forward with notes of peach, jasmine, and honey.
awards: Cup of Excellence winner

name: Gakuyuini Factory
tasting_notes: Intense acidity with flavors of blackberry, grapefruit, and brown sugar.
awards: Kenya Cup of Excellence Top 30 multiple years

name: Elida Estate
tasting_notes: Extremely complex with notes of wild strawberry, bergamot, and cocoa nibs.
awards: Best of Panama Competition winner

name: Don Mayo
tasting_notes: Syrupy and sweet with notes of plum, honey, and vanilla.
awards: Costa Rica Cup of Excellence winner 2018

name: La Hermosa
tasting_notes: Bright and juicy with notes of cherry, caramel, and citrus.
awards: Colombia Cup of Excellence finalist

name: Sítio da Torre
tasting_notes: Smooth and sweet with flavors of peanut butter, milk chocolate, and raisin.
awards: Brazil Cup of Excellence Top 10

name: Finca San Marcos
tasting_notes: Rich and savory with notes of dark chocolate, orange, and subtle spices.
awards: El Salvador Cup of Excellence finalist

name: Finca Vista Hermosa
tasting_notes: Balanced with notes of milk chocolate, red apple, and almond.
awards: Guatemala Cup of Excellence Top 15

name: Gethumbwini Factory
tasting_notes: Complex and juicy with notes of blackcurrant, lime, and brown sugar.
awards: Kenya Cup of Excellence Top 30

name: Nano Challa Cooperative
tasting_notes: Incredibly sweet and floral with notes of peach, jasmine, and honey.
awards: Ethiopia Specialty Coffee Association recognition

name: Finca Deborah
tasting_notes: Wildly complex with notes of strawberry, rose, and champagne.
awards: Used by multiple Barista Champions

name: El Injerto Farm
tasting_notes: Elegant and structured with notes of cherry, dark chocolate, and peach.
awards: Cup of Excellence winner

name: Cedral Alto
tasting_notes: Clean and vibrant with notes of green apple, honey, and white tea.
awards: Costa Rica Excellence Award nominee

name: La Pradera
tasting_notes: Floral and sweet with notes of pink grapefruit, jasmine, and sugarcane.
awards: Colombia Cup of Excellence Top 30 2022

name: Fazenda Conquista
tasting_notes: Heavy body with notes of dark chocolate, toasted nuts, and caramel.
awards: Brazil Cup of Excellence participation award

name: Idido Cooperative
tasting_notes: Bright and tea-like with notes of lemon blossom, apricot, and honey.
awards: Ethiopia Yirgacheffe Cooperative of the Year 2021

name: Kiamabara Factory
tasting_notes: Juicy and vibrant with notes of red currant, lime, and brown sugar.
awards: Kenya Cup of Excellence Top 25

name: Finca La Providencia
tasting_notes: Smooth and balanced with notes of pear, milk chocolate, and walnut.
awards: Nicaragua Cup of Excellence finalist

name: Tanzania Tarime Natural Nº 343
tasting_notes: Luscious raspberry jam and ripe strawberry on the nose, giving way to smooth vanilla cream and buttery shortbread. A natural process showstopper with a long, sweet finish.
awards: Featured in Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 343

name: Ethiopia Aricha Adorsi Nº 346
tasting_notes: Deep molasses sweetness underpins a bright candied lemon note, with a lingering cocoa finish. Classic Yirgacheffe complexity with a dark, syrupy body.
awards: Featured in Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 346

name: Sebastián Ramírez Pink Bourbon Session IPA
tasting_notes: A jaw-dropping anaerobic experiment — tropical mango and candied lime collide with the unmistakable bitterness of hops, like a craft IPA reimagined as a coffee. Wild, precise, and unforgettable.
awards: Rave Coffee Experimental Series #0004

name: Colombia El Carmen Nº 50
tasting_notes: Rich caramel and dark chocolate form the backbone, with a lively red fruit brightness that lifts the cup. Approachable, balanced, and deeply satisfying.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 50

name: Colombia El Carmen Sugarcane Decaf Nº 77
tasting_notes: Remarkably full-bodied for a decaf — rich chocolate and vanilla dominate, with a gentle red fruit sweetness. The sugarcane EA process preserves extraordinary clarity and sweetness.
awards: Rave Coffee Decaf Series Nº 77

name: Indian Monsoon Malabar Coffee Nº 53
tasting_notes: Bold and unapologetic — dark chocolate and cedar wood anchor a distinctive smoky character born from months of exposure to monsoon winds on the Malabar coast. A coffee with ancient character.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 53

name: Guatemala Bosques de San Francisco Nº 52
tasting_notes: Velvety milk chocolate and toffee sweetness, punctuated by a crisp red apple finish. A quintessential Huehuetenango washed with admirable consistency and depth.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 52

name: Brazil Guima Estate Nº 303
tasting_notes: Comfort in a cup — brown sugar sweetness, smooth dark chocolate, and a toasty biscuit finish. The kind of natural Brazilian that works beautifully as both filter and espresso.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 303

name: Rwanda Kirunga Lot 6 Nº 345
tasting_notes: Bursting with fresh strawberry and red liquorice, with a bright mandarin citrus finish. Exceptional clarity and vibrancy typical of the finest Rwandan lots.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 345

name: Peru Arcelia Neyra Nº 359
tasting_notes: Vivid blood orange and ripe nectarine with an intriguing cola sweetness on the finish. A stunning expression of high-altitude Peruvian Arabica by a dedicated female producer.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 359

name: Peru Amigos del Oso Nº 350
tasting_notes: Warming caramel and nutmeg spice over a generous dark chocolate base. A deeply comforting cup that reflects the lush, biodiverse Amazonas landscape where spectacled bears still roam.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 350

name: Mexico Café El Zapoteco Nº 342
tasting_notes: Silky milk chocolate and roasted pecan with a playful glacé cherry sweetness that lingers. Grown by Zapotec indigenous farmers using generations-old cultivation practices.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 342

name: Peru Churupampa Nº 341
tasting_notes: Toasted hazelnut and butter candy sweetness melt into a creamy milk chocolate finish. An exceptionally smooth and gentle cup from high in the Andean cloud forest.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 341

name: Brazil Sertãozinho Lot 574 Nº 319
tasting_notes: Intense chocolate digestive biscuit and roasted hazelnut with a deep molasses sweetness. Full-bodied and structured — an excellent espresso base with serious staying power.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 319

name: Rwanda Vunga Nº 329
tasting_notes: Bright pomegranate and dried berry sweetness, threaded with brown sugar warmth and a smooth dark chocolate finish. Elegant, balanced, and quintessentially Rwandan.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 329

name: Costa Rica Monte Alto Venecia Nº 348
tasting_notes: Exotic and perfumed — Turkish delight and sweet vermouth complexity with a delicate lychee sweetness on the finish. A honeyed Costa Rican that drinks like a dessert wine.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 348

name: Burundi Izuba Nº 334
tasting_notes: Deep black cherry and dark chocolate with a distinctive liquorice root finish. Dense, moody, and complex — 'Izuba' means sun in Kirundi, and this coffee has the intensity to match.
awards: Rave Coffee Single Origin Series Nº 334

name: Choc Ice (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: A Brazil/Costa Rica espresso blend built for richness and balance — think dark chocolate, creamy vanilla, and a cool, clean finish that lives up to its name.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Espresso Blend

name: Red Brick (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: Crisp red apple acidity lifts a foundation of smooth toffee and milk chocolate, finishing with a gentle hazelnut warmth. A classic, dependable espresso blend from Costa Rica and Colombia.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Signature Espresso Blend

name: Zarza (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: A bold anaerobic natural bursting with ripe mango and intense blackcurrant cordial — unmistakably juicy and vivid. One of Square Mile's most expressive and playful seasonal lots.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Seasonal Single Origin

name: Decaf Espresso (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: Lush date and dried fruit sweetness over a silky milk chocolate base, rounded off with a praline nuttiness. An exceptionally satisfying decaf that refuses to compromise on depth.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Decaf Espresso

name: Sweetshop (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: A Guatemala and Kenya blend that lives up to its name — candy-bright acidity, stone fruit sweetness, and a clean, confection-like finish that keeps you reaching for another sip.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Sweetshop Blend

name: Mikuba (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: Bright cherry and fresh raspberry with a lingering toffee sweetness on the finish. A beautiful expression of the Kayanza terroir — vivid, clean, and deeply satisfying.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Seasonal Single Origin

name: The Filter Blend (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: A Uganda and Brazil filter blend with gentle baked apple warmth, toasted almond nuttiness, and a brown sugar sweetness that makes it endlessly drinkable morning to evening.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Filter Blend

name: Ayacucho (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: Delicate golden raisin sweetness blooms into a warming peach compote, with a soft, almost jammy finish. A gentle, contemplative washed Peruvian that rewards a slow pour.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Seasonal Single Origin

name: Gitwe Hill (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: Exuberant blackberry fruit and delicate orange blossom florality, finishing with a fresh grape juiciness. One of Burundi's most elegant and complex washed lots.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Seasonal Single Origin

name: Half Caff (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: A 50/50 caffeinated and decaf Brazil blend — rich fig and dried date sweetness, smooth dark chocolate body, and a toasted hazelnut finish. All the pleasure, half the buzz.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Half Caff Blend

name: Decaf Filter (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: Sunny yellow plum and roasted hazelnut with a sweet praline finish — a natural Brazilian decaf with real personality and filter-optimised clarity.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Decaf Filter

name: Baixadão (Square Mile)
tasting_notes: Vibrant orange zest over a luscious chocolate torte base, with a clean almond nuttiness on the finish. A natural Brazilian with more brightness and lift than you'd expect.
awards: Square Mile Coffee Seasonal Single Origin
